chapter_context.summary: David desires to build a Temple; Nathan delivers the Davidic Covenant, promising that David's son will build the Temple and that his dynasty is eternal; David offers a humble prayer.

outline.text: David Expresses Desire to Build a Temple to Nathan
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Expresses Desire to Build a Temple to Nathan (17:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: The Davidic Covenant Declared; God Promises to Build David a House
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Davidic Covenant Declared; God Promises to Build David a House (17:3-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David's Prayer of Humble Thanksgiving and Praise
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Prayer of Humble Thanksgiving and Praise (17:16-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: Covenant Lineage
key_themes.description: The meticulous genealogies show that God's plan is continuous and links post-exilic Israel back to creation.

key_themes.title: The Throne of David
key_themes.description: David's rise and worship preparations focus on establishing Jerusalem as the central theological hub of the kingdom.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 17, how does seeing God's faithfulness through history encourage you today?
How does this chapter prompt you to dedicate your personal plans and gifts to the LORD's work?

cultural_historical.title: The Post-Exilic Restoration
cultural_historical.text: Chronicles was written for the returning exiles to rebuild their religious identity, focusing heavily on priestly lineages and temple worship.
